title.en: Setting an Alarm for the Morning

phrases.en: Dan will yawn and decide it is finally time for bed.
phrases.zh: 丹会打个哈欠，决定终于该睡觉了。

phrases.en: He will think about his early meeting tomorrow.
phrases.zh: 他会想起明天一大早的会议。

phrases.en: He will pick a sensible wake-up time of six thirty.
phrases.zh: 他会选一个合理的起床时间——六点半。

phrases.en: He will pick up his phone from the desk.
phrases.zh: 他会从书桌上拿起手机。

phrases.en: He will unlock the screen and open the clock app.
phrases.zh: 他会解锁屏幕，打开时钟应用。

phrases.en: He will tap the small plus button to add a new alarm.
phrases.zh: 他会点一下小小的加号按钮，添加一个新闹钟。

phrases.en: He will scroll the dial carefully to six thirty.
phrases.zh: 他会仔细地把表盘滚动到六点半。

phrases.en: He will choose a gentle ringtone instead of a loud one.
phrases.zh: 他会选一个轻柔的铃声，而不是刺耳的那种。

phrases.en: He will turn on the daily repeat so it rings every weekday.
phrases.zh: 他会打开每日重复，这样它每个工作日都会响。

phrases.en: He will save the alarm and check that it is switched on.
phrases.zh: 他会保存闹钟，并确认它已经开启。

phrases.en: He will plug the charging cable into the bottom of the phone.
phrases.zh: 他会把充电线插到手机底部。

phrases.en: He will set the phone face down on the nightstand by the bed.
phrases.zh: 他会把手机正面朝下放在床边的床头柜上。

phrases.en: He will lie down quietly and pull up the warm blanket.
phrases.zh: 他会安静地躺下，拉上温暖的被子。

phrases.en: He will close his eyes, knowing the alarm will wake him on time.
phrases.zh: 他会闭上眼睛，知道闹钟会准时叫醒他。
